xi_cao.name: Xi Cao
xi_cao.summary:
A Maryland competitor, Xi Cao has reached the podium at the ORC level (top-4), currently competing at the U17 level, competing against the best players in the region. Open Regional Championships are a significant step up from local events, and Xi's results show the readiness for top-tier competition.

A true triple-threat, Xi competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #3. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (7 entries) • ORC top-4 finisher.

With 20 different doubles partners across the career, Xi adapts to anyone:
  • Jonathan Wu — 3 events in BD, best: #3

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 1 title across 4 tournaments — a clear sign that Xi is still ascending.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Xi has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Xi isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 11 seeded entries across the career, Xi consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title, Xi is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to dedication, talent, and love for the game.

Xiang competes in both singles and doubles, developing the kind of well-rounded game that pays dividends as competition intensifies at higher levels.

xiaoan_fan.name: Xiaoan Fan
xiaoan_fan.summary:
A Texas competitor, Xiaoan Fan is on an upward trajectory, currently competing at the U13 level, with 13 entries across 6 tournaments. Every tournament adds experience, every match sharpens the game.

Notably, Xiaoan has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

What makes Xiaoan especially formidable is the versatility across all three disciplines. Competing across all disciplines builds a well-rounded game that pure specialists can't replicate.

Xiaoan has competed at both U13 and U15, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Xiaoan brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.

xiner_hu.name: Xiner Hu
xiner_hu.summary:
A California competitor, Xiner Hu is a U.S. Junior National Champion — crowned at the biggest event on the domestic calendar in GD U11. That title represents the culmination of grueling competition against the very best in the country. With 8 career victories across 10 tournaments, Xiner has proven the ability to deliver when it matters most.

Notably, Xiner has entered higher age groups at 2 tournaments, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

Xiner is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 3 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals champion (GD U11) • 7 ORC titles.

The statistics paint a vivid picture — Xiner boasts a clutch 60% win rate in three-game matches (9/15), a biggest win margin of 35 points, a career +970 point differential.

With 8 different doubles partners across the career, Xiner adapts to anyone:
  • Naksh Shetty — 8 events in XD, producing 3 titles together
  • Sophia Z Liu — 3 events in GD, producing 2 titles together

The 2025-2026 season has been outstanding, with 6 podium finishes across 4 tournaments. Xiner is playing some of the best badminton of the career right now.

Xiner has competed at both U11 and U13, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Xiner has competed across 5 different regions (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Xiner consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Xiner's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

Career highlights: Junior Nationals competitor (2 entries).

Xintong has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 10 different players:

Now in the 2nd season of competition, Xintong's journey is gathering momentum with every tournament. The future is bright.

xintong_jiang.name: Xintong Jiang
xintong_jiang.summary:
A California competitor, Xintong Jiang is a multi-time champion on the junior circuit, currently competing at the U15 level, capturing 3 titles across 15 tournaments and 35 entries. Each win is earned through bracket battles against hungry opponents — and Xintong keeps finding ways to come out on top.

Xintong is that rare three-discipline competitor — singles, doubles, and mixed — boasting 1 singles title, 1 doubles title, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Xintong has shown remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 13 different players:
  • Ethan Yip Ly — 3 events, 1 title together in XD
  • Enming Cao — 3 events in XD, best: #4

Having moved up to U15, Xintong is navigating tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment is natural — and with the pedigree Xintong brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

Xintong has been on this journey through U11, U13, U15 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Xintong has competed across 4 different regions (NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

Over 4 seasons and 3 titles, Xintong has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

xinyan_zeng.name: Xinyan Zeng
xinyan_zeng.summary:
A California competitor, Xinyan Zeng has reached the pinnacle of American junior badminton — selected for the U.S. Junior National Team. Winning at the Selection Event (U15 GD, U17 GD) is the ultimate validation: it means being chosen to represent the country on the international stage. With 6 career titles across 25 tournaments, this is a player who delivers when everything is on the line.

Notably, Xinyan has entered higher age groups at 1 tournament, voluntarily taking on older competition — a bold move that reveals both confidence and a hunger for growth.

A true triple-threat, Xinyan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 5 doubles titles, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three formats reveals both individual brilliance and the court sense to thrive alongside a partner.

Career highlights: U.S. Selection Event champion (U15 GD, U17 GD) — selected to represent the USA • Junior Nationals champion (GD U13, GD U17) • 3 ORC titles.

With 21 different doubles partners across the career, Xinyan adapts to anyone:
  • Sophia Jia — 7 events in GD/U17, producing 3 titles together
  • Sydney Li — 4 events in GD/U15, producing 3 titles together
  • Kevin Zhang — a trusted U15/XD partner (4 events, best: #2)

Xinyan has been on this journey through U13, U15, U17, U19 — a multi-age-group career that few can match, accumulating invaluable experience at every level. That depth of competitive history is an asset younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Xinyan has competed across 6 different regions (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 12 seeded entries across the career, Xinyan consistently earns the respect of tournament organizers.

Over 6 seasons and 6 titles, Xinyan has built a legacy that speaks volumes about what sustained effort and competitive fire can achieve.

Xinyang has competed across 4 different regions (NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel the country and test the game against diverse competition from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience, Xinyang brings tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court against the best.
